original_title: Facilities Management & Workplace Services DPS
english_summary: This is a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for facilities management and workplace services aimed at lower value/less complex contracts to attract SME suppliers. It covers maintenance, repairs, installation works, CAFM, help-desk services, and waste management for public sector estates across the UK.
what_they_want: Facilities management and workplace services including maintenance, response repairs, supply and installation works, CAFM (Computer-Aided Facilities Management), help-desk services, and waste management services. NOT medical devices.
eligibility_requirements: Open to suppliers (particularly SMEs targeted) who can provide relevant facilities management services. Available to Central Government Departments and all UK Public Sector Bodies including Local Authorities, Health, Police, Fire and Rescue, Education, Housing, Charities, Not for Profit, Nuclear, Devolved Administrations and International organizations.

original_title: Contract Notice
english_summary: UK Health Security Agency is establishing a 4-year Dynamic Purchasing System (2024-2028) for COVID-19 vaccines for future immunisation campaigns. The DPS has a potential total value of £1 billion (excluding VAT) with no minimum volume commitment, allowing approved suppliers to participate in future tender opportunities.
what_they_want: COVID-19 vaccines (product(s) indicated for protection from COVID-19). No specific model numbers or brands mentioned.
eligibility_requirements: Suitably qualified suppliers must apply and be accepted onto the Dynamic Purchasing System via the Atamis eTendering system. Only suppliers whose DPS application has been approved and accepted at the time an ITT is issued will be eligible to submit tenders. Specific qualifying criteria available through the Atamis system.

original_title: ASC23034 Dynamic Purchasing System for the Provision of Older People Residential and Nursing Care Long Term and Replacement Care Services
english_summary: Worcestershire County Council is establishing a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for the provision of residential and nursing care services for older people. The DPS covers long-term care, short-term replacement care, and emergency short-term replacement care over a possible eight-year term with an estimated lifetime value of £1,107,679,301 excluding VAT.
what_they_want: Residential and nursing care services for older people across three lots: Lot 1 - Long Term Care, Lot 2 - Short Term Replacement Care, and Lot 3 - Emergency Short Term Replacement Care.
eligibility_requirements: Suitably experienced, qualified and appropriately accredited suppliers. Applicants must complete a Standard Selection Questionnaire and upload relevant documents.

original_title: Child and Adolescent Mental Health Services (CAMHS) Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS)
buyer_name: North Staffordshire Combined Healthcare NHS Trust
english_summary: North Staffordshire Combined Healthcare NHS Trust is reopening a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for Child and Adolescent Mental Health Services (CAMHS) covering North Staffordshire and Stoke on Trent. The DPS, which went live in February 2023 for 5 years, allows qualified organizations to bid on future CAMHS service requirements offering face-to-face, digital, and blended delivery approaches.
what_they_want: Child and Adolescent Mental Health Services (CAMHS) for ages 0-25 years (primarily 0-18 years) with multiple delivery modalities including face-to-face, digital, and blended approaches; services must use multi-disciplinary team approaches, be strengths-based and trauma-informed, use appropriate outcome measurement tools, and provide timely, high-quality accessible services for children, young people and their families/carers.
eligibility_requirements: Suitably experienced organisations with expertise in Child and Adolescent Mental Health Services; applicants must indicate their area(s) of expertise and delivery modalities as part of the qualification process; registration must be completed via MLCSU Atamis eTendering System.

original_title: CHC Nursing Home Beds Preferred Provider List- NHS BLMK ICB
buyer_name: NHS Bedfordshire, Luton & Milton Keynes Integrated Care Board
english_summary: NHS Bedfordshire, Luton and Milton Keynes Integrated Care Board is seeking qualified providers of Continuing Healthcare (CHC) nursing home beds to establish a preferred provider list. The service involves providing safe and appropriate care in registered nursing home settings for clients assessed as needing such placement across the BLMK population.
what_they_want: Continuing Healthcare (CHC) nursing home beds services delivered in formal registered nursing home settings for clients requiring specialized care as assessed by the ICB.
eligibility_requirements: Suitably qualified providers of CHC Nursing Homes Beds services under Any Qualified Provider (AQP) framework. Must be able to deliver services across Bedfordshire, Luton and Milton Keynes population areas.

original_title: CHC Nursing Home Beds Preferred Provider List- NHS Bedfordshire, Luton and Milton Keynes ICB
english_summary: NHS Bedfordshire, Luton and Milton Keynes Integrated Care Board is seeking qualified providers of Continuing Healthcare (CHC) nursing home beds to deliver safe and appropriate care in registered nursing home settings. This is an Any Qualified Provider (AQP) contract on a zero value, zero volume basis for an initial 5-year period with optional 2-year extension.
what_they_want: Continuing Healthcare (CHC) nursing home beds services to be delivered in registered nursing home settings across Bedfordshire, Luton and Milton Keynes population.
eligibility_requirements: Suitably qualified providers of CHC Nursing Home Beds services (specific certification and experience requirements to be found in the full procurement documentation on Atamis).

original_title: Nursing Homes Preferred Provider List For Derby and Derbyshire ICB
english_summary: NHS Derby & Derbyshire ICB is establishing a Nursing Home Preferred Provider List on an Any Qualified Provider (AQP) basis, operating like a Dynamic Purchasing System where providers can apply at any time. This is a zero value, zero volume contract for nursing home services with a 5-year initial term plus optional 2-year extension.
what_they_want: Nursing home services to be included on a preferred provider list for Derby and Derbyshire ICB area.
eligibility_requirements: Any qualified providers meeting the requirements specified in the AQP documentation (specific requirements not detailed in this notice but available in Document 11 and service specification on Atamis).

original_title: Mental Health Recovery
english_summary: Rotherham Metropolitan Borough Council is seeking experienced providers to deliver community mental health care and support services through a Flexible Purchasing System (FPS). The services will focus on mental health recovery, personalized social care, and supporting independent living for people with mental ill-health in Rotherham.
what_they_want: Community mental health care and support services including supported living accommodation-based services and day opportunities, all focused on mental health recovery principles. NOT medical devices or equipment.
eligibility_requirements: Experienced providers with capability and capacity to deliver community mental health care and support. Must register with the Social Value Portal and commit to delivering Social Value additionality via all contracts.

original_title: DPS 4 - Supply of community equipment for small moving & handling equipment
english_summary: Croydon Council is procuring small moving and handling equipment through a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for an Integrated Procurement Hub serving multiple local authorities. This is DPS 4, covering 32 categories of small moving and handling equipment, operating as part of a shared community equipment procurement arrangement.
what_they_want: Small moving and handling equipment across 32 categories of community equipment. Specific devices, model numbers, or brands are not mentioned in this notice.
eligibility_requirements: Suppliers must meet the DPS admission criteria set out in the Pre-Qualification Questionnaire (PQQ). Unsuccessful suppliers may re-submit revised PQQs at any time. The system is open to any supplier who meets these criteria throughout the validity period.

notice_id: Croydon Integrated Procurement Hub - DN179859 - DPS3
original_title: DPS 3 - Supply of community equipment for Simple Aids
english_summary: Croydon Council is establishing a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for the supply of simple aids community equipment on behalf of itself and partner local authorities through an Integrated Procurement Hub. This is DPS 3 covering 102 categories of simple aids, with the system remaining open for supplier admission throughout its validity period until November 2026.
what_they_want: Simple aids community equipment across 102 categories. Specific devices, models, or brands are not detailed in this notice.
eligibility_requirements: Suppliers must meet DPS admission criteria set out in the Pre-Qualification Questionnaire (PQQ) to become an "Admitted Supplier." Unsuccessful suppliers may resubmit revised PQQs at any time. Specific certification or company size requirements are not detailed in this notice.

original_title: DPS 2 - Supply of community equipment paediatric and young adults
english_summary: Croydon Council is establishing a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for the supply of paediatric and young adult community equipment on behalf of itself and partner local authorities through the Integrated Procurement Hub. This is DPS_2 consisting of 28 categories of paediatric community equipment, opening September 28, 2016 and valid until October 31, 2026.
what_they_want: 28 categories of paediatric and young adult community equipment for health and social care purposes. Specific device categories, model numbers, and brands are not detailed in this notice.
eligibility_requirements: Suppliers must meet DPS admission criteria set out in the Pre-Qualification Questionnaire (PQQ) to become an "Admitted Supplier." Unsuccessful suppliers may resubmit revised PQQs at any time. The DPS is open to new suppliers throughout its validity period.

original_title: Provision of Non-Emergency Patient Transport Services (NEPTS) for GHFT
english_summary: Gloucestershire Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust is tendering for the provision of Non-Emergency Patient Transport Services (NEPTS) to transport patients who do not require emergency medical attention to and from healthcare facilities.
what_they_want: Non-Emergency Patient Transport Services (NEPTS) for transporting patients to and from hospital appointments and healthcare facilities.

original_title: Occupational Health and Related Services - DPS
english_summary: Crown Commercial Service is establishing a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for occupational health and related services available to public sector, wider public sector, and third sector organizations in the UK. The DPS will be procured under the Light Touch Regime according to PCR 2015 regulations.
what_they_want: Occupational health services, employee assistance programmes, fully managed service, eyecare services, and hearing services.

original_title: Health Checks DPS
english_summary: A Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for NHS Health Check services in West Northamptonshire targeting people aged 40-74 with no prior cardiovascular disease history. The program aims to offer health checks to 49,500 eligible people annually with a target uptake of 27,417-34,650 checks per year over a five-year period from April 2024 to February 2029.
what_they_want: NHS Health Check services for cardiovascular disease risk assessment and management, including early identification and management of cardiovascular risk factors for the eligible population aged 40-74 years without previous CVD history.

original_title: Provision of Best Interest Assessors / Section 12 Doctors - Pseudo Open Framework
english_summary: Hull City Council is seeking tenders for a pseudo open framework agreement to provide Best Interest Assessors and Section 12 Doctors under the Light Touch Regime. The framework will run from April 2025 to March 2028 with an optional 24-month extension, with an estimated value of £200,000 per annum.
what_they_want: Lot 1 - Best Interest Assessors; Lot 2 - Section 12 Doctors

original_title: Functional Electrical Stimulation Supplies and Maintenance
buyer_name: Procurement Assist Limited c/o Integrated Facilities Management Bolton Ltd (iFM Bolton)
english_summary: This is a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for the supply of Functional Electrical Stimulation (FES) supplies and maintenance to UK public healthcare providers over a 60-month period, extended to May 2029. FES is used in general rehabilitation to complement therapy interventions.
what_they_want: Functional Electrical Stimulation (FES) supplies and maintenance equipment for general rehabilitation therapy interventions.

original_title: ASC23043 - Positive Behaviour Support Supported Living Services in Herefordshire & Worcestershire DPS
english_summary: This is a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for Positive Behaviour Support Supported Living Services in Herefordshire and Worcestershire, jointly established by Worcestershire County Council and NHS Worcestershire and Herefordshire Integrated Care Board. Suppliers can apply at any time throughout the DPS term, with evaluations conducted in periodic rounds, and successful applicants will be invited to future mini-competitions or referrals for placements.
what_they_want: NOT FOUND
eligibility_requirements: Suitably experienced, qualified and appropriately accredited suppliers who can meet selection criteria and minimum standards as requested in the SSQ Questionnaire (Stage 1) and pass Evaluation (Stage 2). No maximum number of suppliers.

original_title: National Dynamic Purchasing System for Mobile Diagnostic and Treatment Units
english_summary: A Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for mobile diagnostic and treatment units to increase NHS capacity for elective procedures and reduce waiting lists. Units can be vans or non-permanent cabins deployed at hospital sites or community locations like supermarkets and GP practices.
what_they_want: Mobile diagnostic and/or treatment units in the form of vans or non-permanent units/cabins. Available through three procurement models: Lot 1 - Hire or Lease; Lot 2 - Purchase; Lot 3 - Managed Service. No specific models or brands mentioned.

original_title: P0819A - Application to join the Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) for the Domestic Homicide Reporting
english_summary: Birmingham City Council is establishing a Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS) to procure Independent Author/Chair services to conduct statutory Domestic Homicide Reviews across the West Midlands region. The reviews examine deaths of persons aged 16+ resulting from domestic violence, abuse or neglect to identify lessons learned and prevent future homicides.
what_they_want: Independent Author/Chair services (combined role) to undertake statutory Domestic Homicide Reviews for cases involving deaths of persons aged 16 years and over resulting from domestic violence, abuse or neglect across 7 Local Authorities in the West Midlands (Birmingham, Coventry, Dudley, Sandwell, Solihull, Walsall, and Wolverhampton).

original_title: Nursing Homes Preferred Provider List For Derby and Derbyshire ICB
buyer_name: NHS Derby & Derbyshire Integrated Care Board
english_summary: NHS Derby & Derbyshire Integrated Care Board is establishing a Preferred Provider List for nursing home services using an Any Qualified Provider (AQP) model, similar to a Dynamic Purchasing System. The contract runs for 5 years from August 1, 2021, with an optional 2-year extension, and providers can apply at any time during the contract period.
what_they_want: Nursing home services for the Derby and Derbyshire Integrated Care Board area (no specific medical devices or equipment mentioned).

original_title: Adults with Disabilities Pseudo-DPS
english_summary: This is a 10-year Dynamic Purchasing System for providing home-based and community support services to working-age adults (16+) with disabilities and/or long-term health conditions living in Sheffield, running from March 27, 2023 to March 26, 2033.
what_they_want: Support services for adults with disabilities including assistance within the home and activities/support outside the home for people aged 16 or over with disabilities and/or long-term health conditions.

original_title: Children and Young People Continuing Healthcare
buyer_name: NHS Arden and Greater East Midlands Commissioning Support Unit
english_summary: This is a tender for continuing healthcare services for children and young people with continuing care needs in the Norfolk and Waveney area. The service must be available 24/7, 365 days per year, and will be awarded as a Preferred Provider List on a zero value, zero volume basis for 3 years.
what_they_want: Continuing healthcare services for children and young people (Service Users) who have been determined as having a Continuing Care need, delivered 24 hours a day, 365 days per year in accordance with service specifications and individual care plans.

original_title: Pseudo Dynamic Purchasing System (PDPS) for Rapid Specialist Opinion (RSO) / Referral Management Services (RMS)
buyer_name: NHS NORTH OF ENGLAND COMMISSIONING SUPPORT (HOSTED BY NHS ENGLAND)
english_summary: This tender establishes a Pseudo Dynamic Purchasing System (PDPS) for Rapid Specialist Opinion and Referral Management Services to be used by multiple NHS Clinical Commissioning Groups in the North of England. The system will allow participating NHS organizations to call off services during the contract term.
what_they_want: Rapid Specialist Opinion (RSO) services and Referral Management Services (RMS) for clinical commissioning groups. No specific models or brands mentioned.

original_title: Flexible Procurement System (FPS) for the Provision of Independent Assessors and Reviewers
english_summary: City of Doncaster Council is establishing a Flexible Procurement System for independent assessors and reviewers across seven lots covering mental health assessments, safeguarding reviews, and complaints handling. This is not a procurement of medical devices but rather professional assessment and review services.
what_they_want: NOT FOUND

original_title: Older Peoples Care Homes
english_summary: This is a tender for care home provision services for older people in Sheffield, focused on delivering personalized care that promotes independence, wellbeing, and quality of life while supporting the city's Health and Social Care vision to keep people well at home and reduce hospital admissions.
what_they_want: NOT FOUND
